title: First Meeting
scene: You meet someone for the first time at a language exchange event in Taipei.

lines.speaker: A
lines.zh: 你好！
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo!
lines.english: Hello!
lines.notes: Standard greeting. Literally 'you good'.

lines.speaker: B
lines.zh: 你好！你叫什麼名字？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo! Nǐ jiào shén me míng zi?
lines.english: Hello! What's your name?
lines.notes: 叫 (jiào) means 'to be called'.

lines.speaker: A
lines.zh: 我叫大衛。你呢？
lines.pinyin: Wǒ jiào Dà wèi. Nǐ ne?
lines.english: My name is David. And you?
lines.notes: 你呢 = 'And you?' — a very useful short question.

lines.speaker: B
lines.zh: 我叫小美。很高興認識你！
lines.pinyin: Wǒ jiào Xiǎo měi. Hěn gāo xìng rèn shi nǐ!
lines.english: My name is Xiaomei. Nice to meet you!
lines.notes: 認識 (rèn shi) = to know/meet someone.

lines.speaker: A
lines.zh: 很高興認識你！你是台灣人嗎？
lines.pinyin: Hěn gāo xìng rèn shi nǐ! Nǐ shì Tái wān rén ma?
lines.english: Nice to meet you too! Are you Taiwanese?
lines.notes: Add 嗎 to the end of a statement to make it a yes/no question.

lines.speaker: B
lines.zh: 是的，我是台灣人。你呢？你是哪國人？
lines.pinyin: Shì de, wǒ shì Tái wān rén. Nǐ ne? Nǐ shì nǎ guó rén?
lines.english: Yes, I am Taiwanese. And you? What country are you from?
lines.notes: 哪國人 = 'person of which country'.

lines.speaker: A
lines.zh: 我是美國人。我在學中文。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ shì Měi guó rén. Wǒ zài xué zhōng wén.
lines.english: I'm American. I'm studying Chinese.
lines.notes: 在 + verb = currently doing something (present progressive).

title: Ordering at a Café
scene: You walk into a Taiwanese café and want to order drinks.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 你好，歡迎光臨！請問要喝什麼？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, huān yíng guāng lín! Qǐng wèn yào hē shén me?
lines.english: Hello, welcome! What would you like to drink?
lines.notes: 歡迎光臨 = 'welcome' (formal, used in shops). 請問 = 'may I ask'.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 你好！我要一杯珍珠奶茶。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo! Wǒ yào yī bēi zhēn zhū nǎi chá.
lines.english: Hello! I'd like one bubble milk tea.
lines.notes: 要 = to want. 一杯 = one cup (杯 is the measure word for drinks).

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 好的。甜度要幾分？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de. Tián dù yào jǐ fēn?
lines.english: Sure. What sweetness level would you like?
lines.notes: This is very common in Taiwan! 幾分 = 'how many parts'.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 半糖，謝謝。
lines.pinyin: Bàn táng, xiè xiè.
lines.english: Half sugar, please.
lines.notes: Common options: 全糖 (full), 七分 (70%), 半糖 (50%), 三分 (30%), 微糖 (less), 無糖 (none).

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 冰塊呢？正常冰、少冰，還是去冰？
lines.pinyin: Bīng kuài ne? Zhèng cháng bīng, shǎo bīng, hái shì qù bīng?
lines.english: Ice? Normal ice, less ice, or no ice?
lines.notes: 還是 = 'or' (used in questions with choices).

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 少冰，謝謝。多少錢？
lines.pinyin: Shǎo bīng, xiè xiè. Duō shǎo qián?
lines.english: Less ice, thank you. How much is it?
lines.notes: 多少錢 = 'how much money' — essential phrase!

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 六十元。謝謝惠顧！
lines.pinyin: Liù shí yuán. Xiè xiè huì gù!
lines.english: Sixty dollars. Thank you for your patronage!
lines.notes: 元 = NT dollar. 謝謝惠顧 = formal thank you for shopping.

grammar_points: 我要 + item (I want / I'd like)
Measure word + noun: 一杯水 (one cup of water)
A、B，還是 C? (A, B, or C?)
多少錢 (how much does it cost)

title: Taking the MRT
scene: You're at an MRT station and need help finding the right train.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 不好意思，請問去台北101要搭哪一線？
lines.pinyin: Bù hǎo yì si, qǐng wèn qù Tái běi yī líng yī yào dā nǎ yī xiàn?
lines.english: Excuse me, which line do I take to get to Taipei 101?
lines.notes: 不好意思 = polite way to get someone's attention. 搭 = to take (transport).

lines.speaker: 路人
lines.zh: 你要搭板南線，在市政府站下車。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ yào dā bǎn nán xiàn, zài shì zhèng fǔ zhàn xià chē.
lines.english: You take the Bannan Line, get off at City Hall Station.
lines.notes: 在…下車 = to get off at (a place).

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 謝謝！要在哪裡換車？
lines.pinyin: Xiè xiè! Yào zài nǎ lǐ huàn chē?
lines.english: Thank you! Where do I transfer?
lines.notes: 換車 = to transfer/change trains. Extremely useful!

lines.speaker: 路人
lines.zh: 你現在在淡水信義線，在忠孝復興站換板南線。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ xiàn zài zài dàn shuǐ xìn yì xiàn, zài zhōng xiào fù xīng zhàn huàn bǎn nán xiàn.
lines.english: You're currently on the Danshui-Xinyi Line. Transfer to the Bannan Line at Zhongxiao Fuxing Station.
lines.notes: 現在 = now/currently. In Taiwan, line names are important to know!

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 好的，我知道了。大概要多久？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, wǒ zhī dào le. Dà gài yào duō jiǔ?
lines.english: I see, got it. About how long will it take?
lines.notes: 多久 = how long (time). 大概 = approximately.

lines.speaker: 路人
lines.zh: 大概十五分鐘吧。
lines.pinyin: Dà gài shí wǔ fēn zhōng ba.
lines.english: About fifteen minutes.
lines.notes: 吧 at the end softens the statement — like 'I think'.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 太好了，謝謝你！
lines.pinyin: Tài hǎo le, xiè xiè nǐ!
lines.english: Great, thank you!
lines.notes: 太…了 = too/very (positive here: 'That's great!')

grammar_points: 要 + 搭 + transport (to take a train/bus)
在 + place + 下車 (get off at...)

title: At the Clinic
scene: You're not feeling well and visit a clinic in Taiwan.

lines.speaker: 護士
lines.zh: 你好，請問有什麼不舒服？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, qǐng wèn yǒu shén me bù shū fú?
lines.english: Hello, what seems to be the problem?
lines.notes: 不舒服 = not comfortable = feeling unwell. Very common phrase.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 我頭很痛，而且發燒。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ tóu hěn tòng, ér qiě fā shāo.
lines.english: I have a bad headache and I also have a fever.
lines.notes: 而且 = furthermore/and also. 發燒 = to have a fever (literally 'fire burning').

lines.speaker: 護士
lines.zh: 你從幾天前開始不舒服的？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ cóng jǐ tiān qián kāi shǐ bù shū fú de?
lines.english: How many days ago did you start feeling unwell?
lines.notes: 從…開始 = starting from... 幾天前 = how many days ago.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 大概兩天前。我也有一點咳嗽。
lines.pinyin: Dà gài liǎng tiān qián. Wǒ yě yǒu yī diǎn ké sòu.
lines.english: About two days ago. I also have a bit of a cough.
lines.notes: 一點 = a little bit. 咳嗽 = cough (a difficult word! ké sòu).

lines.speaker: 醫生
lines.zh: 你可能感冒了。我開一些藥給你。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ kě néng gǎn mào le. Wǒ kāi yī xiē yào gěi nǐ.
lines.english: You may have a cold. I'll prescribe some medicine for you.
lines.notes: 可能 = possibly. 開藥 = to prescribe medicine. 給 = to give.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 需要多休息嗎？
lines.pinyin: Xū yào duō xiū xi ma?
lines.english: Do I need to rest more?
lines.notes: 需要 = need to. 多 before a verb = do it more.

lines.speaker: 醫生
lines.zh: 對，多休息、多喝水，三天內應該會好。
lines.pinyin: Duì, duō xiū xi, duō hē shuǐ, sān tiān nèi yīng gāi huì hǎo.
lines.english: Yes, rest more, drink more water, you should feel better within three days.
lines.notes: 應該 = should. 會 = will. 內 = within.

title: Shopping for Clothes
scene: You're shopping for clothes at a market in Taipei.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 歡迎！請問要找什麼？
lines.pinyin: Huān yíng! Qǐng wèn yào zhǎo shén me?
lines.english: Welcome! What are you looking for?
lines.notes: 找 = to look for/find. Very different from 看 (to look at).

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 我想看一下T恤。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng kàn yī xià T xù.
lines.english: I'd like to look at T-shirts.
lines.notes: 一下 softens the request — 'just take a quick look'. Very polite.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 這件怎麼樣？現在特價，只要三百九十九元。
lines.pinyin: Zhè jiàn zěn me yàng? Xiàn zài tè jià, zhǐ yào sān bǎi jiǔ shí jiǔ yuán.
lines.english: How about this one? It's on special, only 399 dollars.
lines.notes: 件 is the measure word for clothing. 特價 = special price/sale.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 很好看！有沒有大一點的？
lines.pinyin: Hěn hǎo kàn! Yǒu méi yǒu dà yī diǎn de?
lines.english: It's nice! Do you have one a bit bigger?
lines.notes: 有沒有 = do you have (question form). adj + 一點 = a bit more adj.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 有，這件大號的。你要試穿嗎？
lines.pinyin: Yǒu, zhè jiàn dà hào de. Nǐ yào shì chuān ma?
lines.english: Yes, this is the large. Would you like to try it on?
lines.notes: 大號 = large (size). 試穿 = to try on clothes.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 好，謝謝。試衣間在哪裡？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo, xiè xiè. Shì yī jiān zài nǎ lǐ?
lines.english: Sure, thanks. Where's the fitting room?
lines.notes: 在哪裡 = where is (something). Pattern: thing + 在哪裡?

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 在那邊，右手邊。
lines.pinyin: Zài nà biān, yòu shǒu biān.
lines.english: Over there, on the right-hand side.
lines.notes: 那邊 = over there. 右手邊 = right-hand side (literally 'right hand side').

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 可以刷卡嗎？
lines.pinyin: Kě yǐ shuā kǎ ma?
lines.english: Can I pay by card?
lines.notes: 刷卡 = to swipe a card = pay by card. 可以嗎 = Is it possible?

grammar_points: Adj + 一點 (a bit more adj)

title: Dining at a Restaurant
scene: You're at a Taiwanese restaurant for dinner.

lines.speaker: 服務生
lines.zh: 幾位？
lines.pinyin: Jǐ wèi?
lines.english: How many people?
lines.notes: 位 = polite measure word for people. 幾 = how many.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 兩位，謝謝。
lines.pinyin: Liǎng wèi, xiè xiè.
lines.english: Two people, thank you.
lines.notes: Note: 兩 (liǎng) = 2 when used with measure words, not 二 (èr).

lines.speaker: 服務生
lines.zh: 請跟我來。這是你們的菜單。
lines.pinyin: Qǐng gēn wǒ lái. Zhè shì nǐ men de cài dān.
lines.english: Please follow me. Here is your menu.
lines.notes: 跟 = to follow. 菜單 = menu.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 謝謝。請問今天有什麼推薦的？
lines.pinyin: Xiè xiè. Qǐng wèn jīn tiān yǒu shén me tuī jiàn de?
lines.english: Thank you. What do you recommend today?
lines.notes: 推薦 = to recommend. A great phrase to use anywhere!

lines.speaker: 服務生
lines.zh: 我們的招牌菜是三杯雞，很多客人喜歡。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ men de zhāo pái cài shì sān bēi jī, hěn duō kè rén xǐ huān.
lines.english: Our signature dish is Three Cup Chicken, many customers love it.
lines.notes: 招牌菜 = signature/house special dish. 三杯雞 is a famous Taiwanese dish!

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 好，我要一份三杯雞，還有一碗滷肉飯。
lines.pinyin: Hǎo, wǒ yào yī fèn sān bēi jī, hái yǒu yī wǎn lǔ ròu fàn.
lines.english: Okay, I'd like one portion of Three Cup Chicken and also one bowl of braised pork rice.
lines.notes: 份 = portion (measure word for food). 碗 = bowl (measure word).

lines.speaker: 服務生
lines.zh: 好的，請問要什麼飲料？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, qǐng wèn yào shén me yǐn liào?
lines.english: Sure, what would you like to drink?
lines.notes: 飲料 = beverage/drink.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 兩杯水就好，謝謝。麻煩你了。
lines.pinyin: Liǎng bēi shuǐ jiù hǎo, xiè xiè. Má fán nǐ le.
lines.english: Just two glasses of water, thank you. Sorry to bother you.
lines.notes: 就好 = just that will do. 麻煩你了 = 'sorry to trouble you' — very polite and common.

grammar_points: 兩 vs 二 — use 兩 with measure words
有什麼…的 (what is there that is...?)

title: Making a Phone Call
scene: You're calling a restaurant to make a reservation.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 你好，這裡是金龍餐廳。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, zhè lǐ shì jīn lóng cān tīng.
lines.english: Hello, this is Golden Dragon Restaurant.
lines.notes: 這裡是 + name = 'This is [name/place]' when answering the phone.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 你好，我想訂位，請問這週六晚上六點還有位子嗎？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, wǒ xiǎng dìng wèi, qǐng wèn zhè zhōu liù wǎn shàng liù diǎn hái yǒu wèi zi ma?
lines.english: Hello, I'd like to make a reservation. Do you still have availability this Saturday evening at 6pm?
lines.notes: 訂位 = to make a reservation (restaurant). 還有 = still have.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 請問幾位？
lines.pinyin: Qǐng wèn jǐ wèi?
lines.english: How many people?

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 四位。
lines.pinyin: Sì wèi.
lines.english: Four people.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 六點的話，我們還有一桌。請問怎麼稱呼？
lines.pinyin: Liù diǎn de huà, wǒ men hái yǒu yī zhuō. Qǐng wèn zěn me chēng hū?
lines.english: For 6pm, we still have one table. May I have your name?
lines.notes: 的話 = in that case. 怎麼稱呼 = 'how shall I address you?' — polite way to ask name.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 我姓陳，陳大衛。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ xìng Chén, Chén Dà wèi.
lines.english: My surname is Chen, David Chen.
lines.notes: 姓 = surname. In Chinese, surname comes FIRST.

lines.speaker: 店員
lines.zh: 好的，陳先生。週六晚上六點，四位，幫您訂好了。
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, Chén xiān shēng. Zhōu liù wǎn shàng liù diǎn, sì wèi, bāng nín dìng hǎo le.
lines.english: Alright, Mr. Chen. Saturday evening at 6pm, for four — your reservation is all set.
lines.notes: 幫您 = to help you (您 is polite 'you'). 訂好了 = successfully reserved.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 謝謝！請問有停車場嗎？
lines.pinyin: Xiè xiè! Qǐng wèn yǒu tíng chē chǎng ma?
lines.english: Thank you! Do you have a parking lot?
lines.notes: 停車場 = parking lot/garage. A common and useful question.

grammar_points: 的話 = in that case / if so
幫 + person + verb (to do something for someone)
Polite 您 vs casual 你

title: Asking for Directions
scene: You're lost near Ximending (西門町) and need directions.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 不好意思，請問西門捷運站怎麼走？
lines.pinyin: Bù hǎo yì si, qǐng wèn xī mén jié yùn zhàn zěn me zǒu?
lines.english: Excuse me, how do I get to Ximen MRT Station?
lines.notes: 怎麼走 = how to go/get there. Essential directions phrase!

lines.speaker: 路人
lines.zh: 你沿著這條路直走，走到紅綠燈右轉。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ yán zhe zhè tiáo lù zhí zǒu, zǒu dào hóng lǜ dēng yòu zhuǎn.
lines.english: Go straight along this road, and turn right at the traffic light.
lines.notes: 沿著 = along. 直走 = go straight. 走到 = walk until you reach.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 右轉以後呢？
lines.pinyin: Yòu zhuǎn yǐ hòu ne?
lines.english: And after turning right?
lines.notes: 以後 = after. 呢 at the end asks 'and then?' or 'what about?'

lines.speaker: 路人
lines.zh: 然後再走大概五分鐘，捷運站就在左手邊。
lines.pinyin: Rán hòu zài zǒu dà gài wǔ fēn zhōng, jié yùn zhàn jiù zài zuǒ shǒu biān.
lines.english: Then walk about five more minutes, the MRT station will be on the left side.
lines.notes: 再 = again/more. 就在 = is right at. 左手邊 = left-hand side.

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 好，謝謝你！走路要多久？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo, xiè xiè nǐ! Zǒu lù yào duō jiǔ?
lines.english: Okay, thank you! How long does it take to walk?
lines.notes: 走路 = to walk. 多久 = how long (time).

lines.speaker: 路人
lines.zh: 大概十分鐘左右。
lines.pinyin: Dà gài shí fēn zhōng zuǒ yòu.
lines.english: About ten minutes or so.
lines.notes: 左右 after a number = 'approximately/about'. Super useful pattern!

lines.speaker: 你
lines.zh: 好的，我知道了。非常謝謝你！
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, wǒ zhī dào le. Fēi cháng xiè xiè nǐ!
lines.english: Got it, I understand. Thank you very much!
lines.notes: 非常 = very/extremely. Stronger than 很.

grammar_points: 走到 + place (walk until you reach...)
再走 + time (walk for more time)
就在 + direction (is right at/on the...)
